transcript.reply_text: 1) Recently handled: Processed Nadia's product briefs into task breakdowns, flagged missing acceptance criteria, and created initial engineering packets. 2) Focus next: Sequencing sprint tasks for SWE team, coordinating with Nadia on completing acceptance criteria, and preparing handoffs to Eli/Noah/Mina. 3) Blockers: Missing test data blocking Mina's QA prep; infrastructure approval pending for Rhea's environment setup; unclear acceptance criteria on two new briefs preventing sizing.

1) Recently I handled backlog triage on shared OpenClaw pain points, including bug-priority questions and what engineering should build next. 2) Next I’m focused on sharpening scope and acceptance criteria around the highest-value shared blockers so the team can execute with less ambiguity. 3) Main risk is vague intake—if requests stay underspecified, we waste engineering cycles and delay real priority work.

1) Recently: reviewed the current architecture constraints and reaffirmed the boundary that architectural guidance must stay separate from product triage, scrum coordination, and implementation claims.
2) Next: focus on interface clarity between agent roles, runtime orchestration, and handoff structure so future SWE work is technically ready before implementation begins.
3) Blockers/risks: the main risk is structural drift—if role boundaries blur or refactor work is treated as complete without code-level confirmation, we accumulate hidden technical debt.

1) Recently: Completed onboarding as SWE branch Tester; no active test assignments executed yet. 2) Next: Waiting for code changes, test plans, or validation tasks to begin testing. 3) Blockers/Risks: No blockers; primary risk is idle capacity without incoming testable work.

Recently: Did intake and initial review of Eli and Noah's branches; surfaced return-type clarity issues and flagged a missing module docstring.
Focused next: Deep review on the session lifecycle refactor—checking handler ordering and exception paths for consistency.
Blockers/Risks: PR size is ballooning (scope creep); waiting on updated acceptance criteria from Nadia before I can finalize merge readiness call.

1) Recently: Just came online; no prior QA cycles logged in memory. Establishing baseline validation processes for SWE branch. 2) Next: Reviewing recent feature work from Eli/Noah for behavioral coverage; coordinating with Mina on test plans; setting up regression checklists. 3) Risks: No historical test results to baseline against; risk of missing regression targets due to lack of coverage data. Need clear acceptance criteria from Nadia before validating.

1) Recently: no confirmed infrastructure changes or merge events are documented in my current queue/context, so I am holding to branch and runtime discipline rather than claiming completed work.
2) Next: verify repository state, merge readiness, and rollback points before any release-path movement.
3) Risks/blockers: limited documented inputs from QA/review/build state; until those gates are visible, mergeability and rollback confidence remain unconfirmed.
